Iowa City Rehab & Health Care

CCN: 165198 · Iowa City, IA 52245 · Johnson County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
3661 Rochester Avenue, Iowa City, IA 52245
Phone
3193517460
Certified beds
89
Avg daily residents
46 (51% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1994-01-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.01 2.94 3.96 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.67 0.51 0.88 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.41 0.65 0.54 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.94 1.77 2.54 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.08 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.76
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.46
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.63
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
RN turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
Administrators departed
3
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.04
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.76
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-08 14 7 9 76 1 76
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-18 32 5 28 244 1 244
Total weighted health score
118.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (46)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-12-15 2026-01-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-12-15 2026-01-17
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-12-15 2026-01-17
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-12-15 2026-01-17
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-10-08 2025-11-30
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-08 2025-05-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-03-04 2025-03-18
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2025-03-04 2025-03-18
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2025-01-14 2025-02-07
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-06
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-03-05 2024-03-25
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-05 2024-03-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-05 2024-03-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-08-01 2023-08-17
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-08-01 2023-08-17
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0572 Give residents a notice of rights, rules, services and charges. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-05-25 2023-08-17
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-05-25 2023-08-17
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2023-05-25 2023-06-30
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2023-05-25 2023-06-30

Methodology & sources
